# Intake — OA7-4L (task-2026-07-13-005)

## Load & protect
- Input: 830 DICOM slices in `input/` (the DICOMs sit directly in `input/`, not an
  `input/OA7-4L/` subdir as the task path implied). Source read-only; loaded via
  `microct_analysis.processing.dicom.load_dicom`, no resampling (deferred to segmentation).
- Volume shape (Z,Y,X) = **(830, 528, 478)**, int16.
- Slice-UID fingerprint `dbeee845b4ea22df`, transfer syntax `1.2.840.10008.1.2.1`.

## Calibration
- Spacing **isotropic 10.5 µm** (0.0105 mm on all three axes) — matches the Scanco
  VivaCT 40 spec. Written to `artifacts/spacing.json`.
- Scanner profile detected: **scanco** (Manufacturer "SCANCO Medical", Model "Model 10").
- **Intensity scaling present and resolved.** DICOM RescaleSlope 0.3911, Intercept −1000,
  so stored values are on a **HU-like rescaled scale**, not raw Scanco attenuation units.
  Peaks confirm it: air ≈ −941, soft-tissue ≈ −35. This matters downstream: the Scanco
  *unitless* thresholds (220/270/320) in `derived_thresholds` are **not** on this scan's
  scale. The **Amira mineralized-bone mask (>2500 HU)** is the scale-appropriate one here,
  and Otsu on this scan lands at **2563** — near-exact agreement. Segmentation should use
  the HU-scale (Amira-style) thresholds, not the Scanco unitless values.

## Histogram — bimodal check → **needs-seeds path** (expected, not a failure)
- The strict two-peak detector flagged only **air (−941)** and **soft-tissue (−35)** peaks;
  it did not register a discrete third bone peak, so `is_bimodal=False`,
  `segmentation_ready=False`, flag `histogram-not-bimodal`.
- **But mineralized bone is clearly separable**: the histogram (see
  `evidence/histogram.png`) shows a distinct bone shoulder/hump at ~5000–6000, and the
  segmentation analysis reports **separability 0.836** at Otsu **2563**. The bone signal is
  real but low-prominence relative to the huge air/soft-tissue peaks, so automatic two-peak
  thresholding won't cleanly pick it.
- Per methodology, this is the **needs-seeds** situation: segmentation should use
  marker-based watershed with seeds (Amira workflow: >2500 HU mask, watershed markers
  3000–5000 HU) rather than a naive automatic threshold. Recorded here; intake does **not**
  fail. `stage_report`: confidence **medium**, recommended_action **flag**.

## Review render
- `artifacts/intake/volume.json`: volume-only downsampled grayscale render, grid
  [118,105,119], uint8 window-normalized (1–99.5 pct), 1.97 MB (<5 MB). Visualization only
  — the analysis volume is NOT resampled here.

## Handoff to segmentation
- Expect the **needs-seeds** path. Use HU-scale thresholds (bone mask >~2500 HU, confirmed
  by Otsu 2563), not Scanco unitless. Spacing 10.5 µm isotropic in `spacing.json`.

}

<script>
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// TEMPLATE-OWNED CORE RENDERING — DO NOT EDIT PER-PHASE.
//
// Everything in this script block (buildMesh, scene/camera setup, the layers
// panel, projection/overlay, tour, tabs) is owned by the template. Per-phase
// authors inject ONLY the data blocks above (window.REVIEW_MANIFEST,
// REVIEW_EVIDENCE, REVIEW_GEOMETRY, optional REVIEW_VOLUME) and captions.
//
// Why: when a rendering bug shipped here, authors patched it differently per
// phase (flatten3 vs flat3 vs no patch), so the five phase sites diverged and
// some meshes rendered as NaN/invisible. Rendering fixes belong HERE, once —
// buildMesh already accepts both nested (N,3) and flat vertex/face arrays.
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
//
// Review-artifact 3D app (fixture). Read-only spatial review: rotate, examine,
// walk the guided landmark tour, read the evidence. No exfil surfaces, no
// inline handlers, no runtime data loading, no eval (G5/I3), no landmark
// editing (the artifact is evidence, not an editor). All data is present as
// window globals; three.js + OrbitControls are inlined above. The 3D scene is
// the PRIMARY view: OrbitControls drag rotates the camera.
(function () {
  "use strict";

  var THREE = window.THREE;
  var MANIFEST = window.REVIEW_MANIFEST;
  var GEO = window.REVIEW_GEOMETRY || {};
  var EV = window.REVIEW_EVIDENCE || {};
  var VOLUME = window.REVIEW_VOLUME || null;
  var HAS_VOLUME = !!VOLUME; // volume-only phases render grayscale slice planes

  // --- Render mode -----------------------------------------------------------
  // One template serves every micro-CT phase. Which parts render is driven by
  // which verified data the phase actually shipped — never by fabricating the
  // rest:
  //   • mesh mode        — REVIEW_GEOMETRY.meshes present (segmentation onward)
  //   • volume-only mode — no meshes but a REVIEW_VOLUME (intake, pre-segmentation)
  //   • landmarks        — REVIEW_EVIDENCE.landmarks non-empty (landmarks/measurement)
  //   • evidence/measurement — REVIEW_EVIDENCE.decisive[0] present (measurement)
  // Absent data is simply not drawn; the 3D OrbitControls scene is always the
  // primary view.
  var meshNames = GEO && GEO.meshes ? Object.keys(GEO.meshes) : [];
  var HAS_MESH = meshNames.length > 0;
  var landmarkList = EV && Array.isArray(EV.landmarks) ? EV.landmarks : [];
  var HAS_LANDMARKS = landmarkList.length > 0;
  var decisive = EV && Array.isArray(EV.decisive) && EV.decisive[0] ? EV.decisive[0] : null;
  var HAS_EVIDENCE = !!decisive;

  // --- Header + document title ---------------------------------------------
  // The tab/window title is derived from the manifest at runtime so it can
  // never be the static template placeholder. Phase name comes from
  // MANIFEST.phase when present, else the provenance key (produced_from).
  var PHASE_NAME = MANIFEST.phase || Object.keys(MANIFEST.produced_from || {})[0] || "";
  document.title = "LabRat review — " + MANIFEST.sample_id + (PHASE_NAME ? " — " + PHASE_NAME : "");
  document.getElementById("sample-title").textContent = "Review " + MANIFEST.sample_id;

  // Orientation legend: ONE canonical anatomical convention for every phase,
  // rendered by the template (never per-phase authored text). A manifest may
  // override via MANIFEST.orientation = ["X …", "Y …", "Z …"] if a protocol
  // genuinely uses a different frame.
  (function () {
    var conv = Array.isArray(MANIFEST.orientation) && MANIFEST.orientation.length === 3
      ? MANIFEST.orientation
      : ["X lateral", "Y proximal", "Z anterior"];
    document.getElementById("orient-text").textContent = conv.join(" · ");
  })();
  // Provenance collapses to a short, hoverable link (full hash off-screen on mobile).
  (function () {
    // Phases differ in what they were produced from: measurement ships a
    // results.json, segmentation a geometry.json, intake a volume.json. Show
    // whichever provenance the manifest actually carries.
    var pf = MANIFEST.produced_from || {};
    var src = pf.measurement || pf.geometry || pf.volume || pf[Object.keys(pf)[0]];
    var p = document.getElementById("sample-source");
    if (!src) { p.textContent = ""; return; }
    var name = src.split("/").pop().split("@")[0];
    var hash = (src.split("@")[1] || "").slice(0, 10);
    var a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = "#";
    a.title = "from " + src;
    a.textContent = "from " + name + (hash ? " @" + hash + "…" : "");
    a.addEventListener("click", function (e) { e.preventDefault(); });
    p.textContent = "";
    p.appendChild(a);
  })();

  // --- Human-readable landmark names --------------------------------------
  function humanize(id) {
    return id.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, function (c) { return c.toUpperCase(); });
  }

  var STATE_COLOR = { pass: "#1f8a4c", concern: "#b26a00", fail: "#b3261e" };

  // --- Evidence banner: big result, cutoff, delta, status -----------------
  function renderBanner() {
    var banner = document.getElementById("evidence-banner");
    banner.textContent = "";
    var state = STATE_COLOR[decisive.state] || "#4b5563";

    var metric = document.createElement("div");
    metric.className = "ev-metric";
    metric.style.setProperty("--state", state);
    var label = document.createElement("span");
    label.className = "ev-label";
    label.textContent = decisive.label;
    var val = document.createElement("span");
    val.className = "ev-value";
    val.textContent = decisive.value.toFixed(2);
    var unit = document.createElement("span");
    unit.className = "ev-unit";
    unit.textContent = " " + decisive.unit;
    val.appendChild(unit);
    var cut = document.createElement("span");
    cut.className = "ev-cut";
    var oa = decisive.cutoff.oa_above;
    var delta = decisive.value - oa;
    var side = delta >= 0 ? "above" : "below";
    cut.appendChild(document.createTextNode("OA cutoff ≥ " + oa.toFixed(2) + " · "));
    var b = document.createElement("b");
    b.textContent = (delta >= 0 ? "+" : "−") + Math.abs(delta).toFixed(2) + " " + side + " cutoff";
    cut.appendChild(b);
    metric.appendChild(label);
    metric.appendChild(val);
    metric.appendChild(cut);
    banner.appendChild(metric);

    var status = document.createElement("div");
    status.className = "ev-status";
    status.style.setProperty("--state", state);
    var icon = document.createElement("span");
    icon.className = "ev-status-icon";
    icon.setAttribute("role", "img");
    var needsReview = decisive.requires_human_review;
    icon.setAttribute("aria-label", needsReview ? "Review required" : "Pass");
    icon.textContent = needsReview ? "⚠" : "✓";
    var stext = document.createElement("span");
    stext.className = "ev-status-text";
    var main = document.createElement("span");
    main.className = "ev-status-main";
    main.textContent = needsReview ? "Review required" : "Within tolerance";
    var sub = document.createElement("span");
    sub.className = "ev-status-sub";
    var conf = (EV.interpretation && EV.interpretation.confidence) || "";
    sub.textContent = conf ? humanize(conf) + " confidence" : "";
    stext.appendChild(main);
    stext.appendChild(sub);
    status.appendChild(icon);
    status.appendChild(stext);
    banner.appendChild(status);
  }
  // Phases without a decisive measurement (intake volume, segmentation mesh)
  // show a neutral phase note instead of a metric banner — never a fabricated
  // number.
  if (HAS_EVIDENCE) {
    renderBanner();
  } else {
    var banner = document.getElementById("evidence-banner");
    banner.textContent = "";
    var note = document.createElement("div");
    note.className = "ev-metric";
    note.style.setProperty("--state", "#4b5563");
    var nlabel = document.createElement("span");
    nlabel.className = "ev-label";
    nlabel.textContent = HAS_MESH ? "Segmentation review — 3D structures" : "Intake review — grayscale volume";
    var nsub = document.createElement("span");
    nsub.className = "ev-cut";
    nsub.textContent = "No quantitative measurement in this phase yet.";
    note.appendChild(nlabel);
    note.appendChild(nsub);
    banner.appendChild(note);
  }

  var ratioEl = document.getElementById("ratio-value");

  // --- Three.js scene ------------------------------------------------------
  var canvas = document.getElementById("scene3d-canvas");
  var renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ canvas: canvas, antialias: true, alpha: true });
  renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio || 1, 2));
  var scene = new THREE.Scene();
  var FOV = 42;
  var camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(FOV, 1, 0.05, 1000);
  var fovRad = FOV * Math.PI / 180;

  var controls = new THREE.OrbitControls(camera, canvas);
  controls.enableDamping = true;
  controls.dampingFactor = 0.09;
  controls.enablePan = false; // read-only review: rotate + zoom only

  scene.add(new THREE.AmbientLight(0xffffff, 0.7));
  var key = new THREE.DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 0.85);
  key.position.set(3, 6, 5);
  scene.add(key);
  var fill = new THREE.DirectionalLight(0xbcd0ff, 0.35);
  fill.position.set(-4, -2, -3);
  scene.add(fill);
  var rim = new THREE.DirectionalLight(0xffffff, 0.3);
  rim.position.set(0, -3, -6);
  scene.add(rim);

  // Flatten an array of [a,b,c] triples — the numpy (N,3).tolist() shape that
  // real phase geometry ships — into a flat typed array of length 3N. Also
  // accepts an already-flat numeric array (length divisible by 3). Returns
  // null for empty/malformed/non-finite input: three.js coerces nested arrays
  // and bad values to NaN, which renders an invisible mesh and logs
  // "Computed radius is NaN", so malformed structures are skipped instead.
  function flattenTriples(arr, Ctor) {
    if (!Array.isArray(arr) || arr.length === 0) return null;
    var out, i, t, v;
    if (Array.isArray(arr[0])) {          // nested (N,3) shape
      out = new Ctor(arr.length * 3);
      for (i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
        t = arr[i];
        if (!Array.isArray(t) || t.length !== 3 ||
            !isFinite(t[0]) || !isFinite(t[1]) || !isFinite(t[2])) return null;
        out[i * 3] = t[0]; out[i * 3 + 1] = t[1]; out[i * 3 + 2] = t[2];
      }
      return out;
    }
    if (arr.length % 3 !== 0) return null; // flat shape
    out = new Ctor(arr.length);
    for (i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) {
      v = arr[i];
      if (typeof v !== "number" || !isFinite(v)) return null;
      out[i] = v;
    }
    return out;
  }

  // Bone meshes — raised opacity + inverted-hull outline for clear contours.
  // Returns null (structure skipped, no throw, no NaN) when the geometry is
  // empty or malformed.
  function buildMesh(md, color) {
    var positions = md ? flattenTriples(md.vertices, Float32Array) : null;
    var index = md ? flattenTriples(md.faces, Uint32Array) : null;
    if (!positions || !index) return null;
    var geo = new THREE.BufferGeometry();
    geo.setAttribute("position", new THREE.BufferAttribute(positions, 3));
    geo.setIndex(new THREE.BufferAttribute(index, 1));
    geo.computeVertexNormals();
    var group = new THREE.Group();
    var mat = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({
      color: color, transparent: true, opacity: 0.84,
      roughness: 0.55, metalness: 0.03, side: THREE.FrontSide,
      depthWrite: true,
    });
    group.add(new THREE.Mesh(geo, mat));
    // Silhouette outline: dark back-faces slightly enlarged.
    var outlineMat = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ color: 0x10141a, side: THREE.BackSide });
    var outline = new THREE.Mesh(geo, outlineMat);
    outline.scale.multiplyScalar(1.012);
    group.add(outline);
    return group;
  }

  // Build the three center orthogonal slice planes from a REVIEW_VOLUME
  // (shape [nz, ny, nx], grayscale_b64 = window-normalized uint8, row-major
  // z→y→x). Returns a Group centered on the origin, or null if the volume is
  // malformed. Grayscale is baked into the texture (MeshBasicMaterial), so the
  // planes read the same under any lighting.
  function buildVolumePlanes(vol) {
    if (!vol || !Array.isArray(vol.shape) || typeof vol.grayscale_b64 !== "string") return null;
    var nz = vol.shape[0], ny = vol.shape[1], nx = vol.shape[2];
    if (!(nz > 0 && ny > 0 && nx > 0)) return null;
    var sp = Array.isArray(vol.spacing_mm) ? vol.spacing_mm : [1, 1, 1];
    var sz = sp[0] || 1, sy = sp[1] || 1, sx = sp[2] || 1;

    // Decode base64 → Uint8Array (one byte per voxel).
    var bin;
    try { bin = atob(vol.grayscale_b64); } catch (e) { return null; }
    var expected = nz * ny * nx;
    if (bin.length < expected) return null;
    var data = new Uint8Array(expected);
    for (var i = 0; i < expected; i++) data[i] = bin.charCodeAt(i);

    // Build an RGBA DataTexture for one slice plane.
    function sliceTexture(w, h, sample) {
      var rgba = new Uint8Array(w * h * 4);
      for (var yy = 0; yy < h; yy++) {
        for (var xx = 0; xx < w; xx++) {
          var g = sample(xx, yy);
          var o = (yy * w + xx) * 4;
          rgba[o] = g; rgba[o + 1] = g; rgba[o + 2] = g; rgba[o + 3] = 255;
        }
      }
      var tex = new THREE.DataTexture(rgba, w, h, THREE.RGBAFormat);
      tex.needsUpdate = true;
      return tex;
    }
    function plane(w, h, tex) {
      return new THREE.Mesh(
        new THREE.PlaneGeometry(w, h),
        new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ map: tex, side: THREE.DoubleSide }),
      );
    }
    function vox(z, y, x) { return data[z * ny * nx + y * nx + x]; }

    var z0 = nz >> 1, y0 = ny >> 1, x0 = nx >> 1;
    var group = new THREE.Group();

    // Axial (constant z): nx × ny in the XY plane.
    var axial = plane(nx * sx, ny * sy, sliceTexture(nx, ny, function (x, y) { return vox(z0, y, x); }));
    axial.position.set(0, 0, (z0 - nz / 2) * sz);
    group.add(axial);

    // Coronal (constant y): nx × nz in the XZ plane.
    var coronal = plane(nx * sx, nz * sz, sliceTexture(nx, nz, function (x, z) { return vox(z, y0, x); }));
    coronal.rotation.x = -Math.PI / 2;
    coronal.position.set(0, (y0 - ny / 2) * sy, 0);
    group.add(coronal);

    // Sagittal (constant x): ny × nz in the YZ plane.
    var sagittal = plane(ny * sy, nz * sz, sliceTexture(ny, nz, function (y, z) { return vox(z, y, x0); }));
    sagittal.rotation.y = Math.PI / 2;
    sagittal.position.set((x0 - nx / 2) * sx, 0, 0);
    group.add(sagittal);

    return group;
  }
  // Canonical structure colors; any other structure gets a neutral bone tone.
  var MESH_COLORS = { femur: 0xe4c98f, tibia: 0x9fb6cf }; // warm tan / cool blue
  var boneGroup = new THREE.Group();
  // name → { group, colorHex } for every structure that built successfully;
  // the layers panel drives per-structure visibility through this map.
  var structureMeshes = {};
  if (HAS_MESH) {
    meshNames.forEach(function (name) {
      var color = MESH_COLORS[name] || 0xbfc8d4;
      var m = buildMesh(GEO.meshes[name], color);
      if (!m) return; // empty/malformed structure: skipped, never NaN
      boneGroup.add(m);
      structureMeshes[name] = { group: m, colorHex: "#" + color.toString(16).padStart(6, "0") };
    });
    scene.add(boneGroup);
  }

  // --- Volume-only mode: grayscale orthogonal slice planes -----------------
  // Intake ships a downsampled REVIEW_VOLUME but no mesh. Render the three
  // center slice planes (axial/coronal/sagittal) as textured planes inside the
  // same OrbitControls scene — a genuine 3D grayscale volume view, no fabricated
  // geometry.
  var volumeGroup = null;
  if (!HAS_MESH && HAS_VOLUME) {
    volumeGroup = buildVolumePlanes(VOLUME);
    if (volumeGroup) scene.add(volumeGroup);
  }

  // Orientation axes, tucked into a corner near the joint.
  var axes = new THREE.AxesHelper(1.0);
  scene.add(axes);

  // --- Landmarks: constant-screen-size markers + outline -------------------
  var landmarks = {};
  // landmarkList is resolved at the top (empty when the phase has no landmarks).
  var order = landmarkList.map(function (l) { return l.name; });
  var MARKER_GEO = new THREE.SphereGeometry(1, 20, 16); // unit sphere; scaled per frame
  var OUTLINE_GEO = new THREE.SphereGeometry(1.35, 18, 14);
  var HIT_GEO = new THREE.SphereGeometry(2.6, 12, 10);
  var hitMeshes = [];

  landmarkList.forEach(function (lm) {
    var col = lm.color ? (lm.color[0] << 16) | (lm.color[1] << 8) | lm.color[2] : 0xff4d4d;
    var pos = new THREE.Vector3(lm.position[0], lm.position[1], lm.position[2]);
    var marker = new THREE.Group();
    marker.position.copy(pos);

    var outline = new THREE.Mesh(OUTLINE_GEO, new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ color: 0x0e1218 }));
    marker.add(outline);
    var core = new THREE.Mesh(MARKER_GEO, new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({
      color: col, emissive: col, emissiveIntensity: 0.4, roughness: 0.35, metalness: 0,
    }));
    marker.add(core);
    scene.add(marker);

    // Invisible larger hit target for raycast (easier to click).
    var hit = new THREE.Mesh(HIT_GEO, new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ visible: false }));
    hit.position.copy(pos);
    hit.userData.landmark = lm.name;
    scene.add(hit);
    hitMeshes.push(hit);

    // DOM label + leader (built once, positioned each frame).
    var hex = "#" + col.toString(16).padStart(6, "0");
    var labelEl = document.createElement("div");
    labelEl.className = "lm-label";
    labelEl.style.setProperty("--lm", hex);
    labelEl.textContent = humanize(lm.name);
    labelEl.style.display = "none";
    labelEl.addEventListener("click", function () { goStep(order.indexOf(lm.name)); });
    document.getElementById("label-layer").appendChild(labelEl);

    var leaderEl = document.createElementNS("http://www.w3.org/2000/svg", "line");
    leaderEl.setAttribute("stroke", hex);
    leaderEl.setAttribute("stroke-width", "1.4");
    leaderEl.setAttribute("opacity", "0.85");
    leaderEl.style.display = "none";
    document.getElementById("leader-layer").appendChild(leaderEl);

    var ringEl = document.createElement("div");
    ringEl.className = "sel-ring";
    ringEl.style.setProperty("--lm", hex);
    ringEl.style.display = "none";
    document.getElementById("label-layer").appendChild(ringEl);

    landmarks[lm.name] = {
      marker: marker, core: core, outline: outline, pos: pos,
      label: labelEl, leader: leaderEl, ring: ringEl, hex: hex, data: lm,
      hit: hit, hidden: false,
    };
  });

  // Confidence legend — only meaningful when the phase actually has landmarks
  // (intake/segmentation have none, so the legend would be dead chrome).
  (function buildLegend() {
    if (!HAS_LANDMARKS) return;
    var el = document.getElementById("conf-legend");
    var rows = [
      ["ring", "#fff", "Selected landmark"],
      ["", "#7fd0a0", "High confidence"],
      ["", "#e6b04d", "Review needed"],
    ];
    rows.forEach(function (r) {
      var span = document.createElement("span");
      var dot = document.createElement("span");
      dot.className = "conf-dot" + (r[0] ? " " + r[0] : "");
      dot.style.background = r[1];
      span.appendChild(dot);
      span.appendChild(document.createTextNode(r[2]));
      el.appendChild(span);
    });
  })();

  // --- Measurement lines (static evidence) + DOM mm badges ----------------
  var lines = {};
  function lmPos(name) {
    var l = landmarks[name];
    return l ? l.pos : new THREE.Vector3();
  }
  // Measurement lines only exist on a decisive measurement (they connect named
  // landmarks); phases without evidence draw none.
  var measurementLines = HAS_EVIDENCE && Array.isArray(decisive.measurement_lines) ? decisive.measurement_lines : [];
  measurementLines.forEach(function (ml) {
    var a = lmPos(ml.from), b = lmPos(ml.to);
    var geom = new THREE.BufferGeometry().setFromPoints([a.clone(), b.clone()]);
    var mat = new THREE.LineBasicMaterial({ color: 0xffd24d, transparent: true, opacity: 0.9 });
    var line = new THREE.Line(geom, mat);
    line.renderOrder = 5;
    scene.add(line);
    var badge = document.createElement("div");
    badge.className = "mm-badge";
    badge.textContent = ml.value_mm.toFixed(2) + " mm";
    badge.style.display = "none";
    document.getElementById("label-layer").appendChild(badge);
    lines[ml.id] = { line: line, badge: badge, from: ml.from, to: ml.to, mid: a.clone().add(b).multiplyScalar(0.5) };
  });
  var len0 = 0, wid0 = 0;
  measurementLines.forEach(function (ml) {
    if (ml.id === "femoral_length") len0 = ml.value_mm;
    if (ml.id === "femoral_width") wid0 = ml.value_mm;
  });
  if (len0 && wid0) ratioEl.textContent = "W/L = " + (wid0 / len0).toFixed(2);

  // --- Layers panel: the interactive key -----------------------------------
  // A docked panel, consistent across every phase, listing the scene's actual
  // toggleable elements grouped by kind. Each row toggles the visibility of
  // exactly one element (a structure mesh, a landmark's marker+label, or a
  // measurement line+label) — replacing the old all-or-nothing hide-labels.
  // A group is rendered only when the phase has those elements.
  (function buildLayersPanel() {
    var panel = document.getElementById("layers-panel");
    var groups = [];

    // STRUCTURES — one row per successfully built mesh.
    var structNames = Object.keys(structureMeshes);
    if (structNames.length) {
      groups.push({
        title: "Structures",
        rows: structNames.map(function (name) {
          var s = structureMeshes[name];
          return {
            swatch: s.colorHex, dot: false, name: humanize(name),
            visible: function () { return s.group.visible; },
            toggle: function () { s.group.visible = !s.group.visible; },
          };
        }),
      });
    }

    // LANDMARKS — one row per landmark; toggles marker + label + hit target.
    if (HAS_LANDMARKS) {
      groups.push({
        title: "Landmarks",
        rows: landmarkList.map(function (lm) {
          var L = landmarks[lm.name];
          return {
            swatch: L.hex, dot: true, name: humanize(lm.name),
            visible: function () { return !L.hidden; },
            toggle: function () {
              L.hidden = !L.hidden;
              L.marker.visible = !L.hidden;
              if (L.hit) L.hit.userData.hidden = L.hidden;
              // label/leader/ring are hidden by updateOverlay when L.hidden.
            },
          };
        }),
      });
    }

    // MEASUREMENTS — one row per line; toggles the line + its mm badge.
    var lineIds = Object.keys(lines);
    if (lineIds.length) {
      groups.push({
        title: "Measurements",
        rows: lineIds.map(function (id) {
          var Ln = lines[id];
          return {
            swatch: "#ffd24d", dot: false, name: humanize(id),
            visible: function () { return !Ln.hidden; },
            toggle: function () {
              Ln.hidden = !Ln.hidden;
              Ln.line.visible = !Ln.hidden;
            },
          };
        }),
      });
    }

    if (!groups.length) return; // nothing toggleable (e.g. bare volume): stay hidden
    panel.hidden = false;

    groups.forEach(function (g) {
      var h = document.createElement("div");
      h.className = "layers-group-title";
      h.textContent = g.title;
      panel.appendChild(h);
      g.rows.forEach(function (row) {
        var btn = document.createElement("button");
        btn.type = "button";
        btn.className = "layer-row";
        var sw = document.createElement("span");
        sw.className = "layer-swatch" + (row.dot ? " dot" : "");
        sw.style.background = row.swatch;
        var nm = document.createElement("span");
        nm.className = "layer-name";
        nm.textContent = row.name;
        var st = document.createElement("span");
        st.className = "layer-state";
        function sync() {
          var on = row.visible();
          btn.classList.toggle("off", !on);
          st.textContent = on ? "◉" : "○";
          btn.setAttribute("aria-pressed", on ? "true" : "false");
        }
        btn.appendChild(sw);
        btn.appendChild(nm);
        btn.appendChild(st);
        btn.addEventListener("click", function () { row.toggle(); sync(); });
        sync();
        panel.appendChild(btn);
      });
    });
  })();

  // --- Camera framing ------------------------------------------------------
  // Fit whatever the phase actually renders: the landmark cloud when present
  // (fit the joint, not the long shaft), else the mesh, else the volume planes.
  var lmBox = new THREE.Box3();
  if (HAS_LANDMARKS) {
    landmarkList.forEach(function (lm) {
      lmBox.expandByPoint(new THREE.Vector3(lm.position[0], lm.position[1], lm.position[2]));
    });
  } else if (HAS_MESH) {
    lmBox.setFromObject(boneGroup);
  } else if (volumeGroup) {
    lmBox.setFromObject(volumeGroup);
  }
  if (lmBox.isEmpty()) lmBox.set(new THREE.Vector3(-1, -1, -1), new THREE.Vector3(1, 1, 1));
  var jointCenter = lmBox.getCenter(new THREE.Vector3());
  var jointSize = lmBox.getSize(new THREE.Vector3());
  var jointRadius = Math.max(jointSize.length() * 0.5, 0.9);
  var fitDist = jointRadius / Math.sin(fovRad / 2) * 1.35;

  // Constrain the orbit so you can't fly into a useless close/edge-on pose.
  controls.minDistance = fitDist * 0.5;
  controls.maxDistance = fitDist * 1.9;
  controls.minPolarAngle = Math.PI * 0.16;
  controls.maxPolarAngle = Math.PI * 0.84;

  var DEFAULT_DIR = new THREE.Vector3(0.35, 0.28, 1).normalize();
  // Canonical, legible per-landmark viewing directions (mostly anterior).
  var POSE_DIR = {
    trochlear_groove_top: [0.2, 0.35, 1],
    intercondylar_notch: [0.2, -0.12, 1],
    lateral_condylar_edge: [-0.55, 0.12, 0.9],
    medial_condylar_edge: [0.55, 0.12, 0.9],
  };
  function poseForLandmark(name) {
    var p = lmPos(name);
    var d = POSE_DIR[name] || [0.35, 0.28, 1];
    var dir = new THREE.Vector3(d[0], d[1], d[2]).normalize();
    var target = p.clone().lerp(jointCenter, 0.32);
    var dist = fitDist * 0.74;
    return { pos: target.clone().add(dir.clone().multiplyScalar(dist)), target: target };
  }
  function defaultPose() {
    return { pos: jointCenter.clone().add(DEFAULT_DIR.clone().multiplyScalar(fitDist)), target: jointCenter.clone() };
  }
  function dirPose(dx, dy, dz) {
    var dir = new THREE.Vector3(dx, dy, dz).normalize();
    return { pos: jointCenter.clone().add(dir.multiplyScalar(fitDist)), target: jointCenter.clone() };
  }

  // Place axes helper at the lower-anterior corner of the joint box.
  axes.position.set(lmBox.min.x - 0.4, lmBox.min.y - 0.4, lmBox.max.z + 0.2);

  var start = defaultPose();
  camera.position.copy(start.pos);
  controls.target.copy(start.target);
  controls.update();

  // --- Camera fly-to (lerp) ------------------------------------------------
  var camLerp = null;
  function flyTo(pose) {
    camLerp = {
      t: 0, from: camera.position.clone(), to: pose.pos.clone(),
      tFrom: controls.target.clone(), tTo: pose.target.clone(),
    };
  }
  function snapZoom(factor) {
    var dir = camera.position.clone().sub(controls.target);
    var dist = dir.length();
    var next = Math.max(controls.minDistance, Math.min(controls.maxDistance, dist * factor));
    camera.position.copy(controls.target.clone().add(dir.setLength(next)));
    controls.update();
  }

  // --- Overlay: project markers/labels/badges to screen each frame --------
  var currentName = order[0];
  var hoverName = null;
  var showAll = false;

  function project(vec) {
    var v = vec.clone().project(camera);
    var w = canvas.clientWidth || 1, h = canvas.clientHeight || 1;
    return { x: (v.x * 0.5 + 0.5) * w, y: (-v.y * 0.5 + 0.5) * h, z: v.z, w: w, h: h };
  }
  function clamp(v, lo, hi) { return Math.max(lo, Math.min(hi, v)); }

  function updateMarkerScales() {
    var h = canvas.clientHeight || 1;
    Object.keys(landmarks).forEach(function (name) {
      var L = landmarks[name];
      var dist = camera.position.distanceTo(L.pos);
      var worldPerPx = 2 * dist * Math.tan(fovRad / 2) / h;
      var selected = name === currentName;
      var px = selected ? 7 : 5.5;
      L.marker.scale.setScalar(worldPerPx * px);
      L.core.material.emissiveIntensity = selected ? 0.95 : (name === hoverName ? 0.6 : 0.3);
      L.core.material.opacity = selected ? 1 : 0.85;
      L.core.material.transparent = true;
    });
  }

  function updateOverlay() {
    if (!HAS_LANDMARKS) return; // no markers/labels/badges to project
    var w = canvas.clientWidth || 1, h = canvas.clientHeight || 1;
    var narrow = w < 520;
    var m = 8; // edge margin
    Object.keys(landmarks).forEach(function (name) {
      var L = landmarks[name];
      if (L.hidden) { // toggled off in the layers panel: hide all overlays
        L.label.style.display = "none";
        L.leader.style.display = "none";
        L.ring.style.display = "none";
        return;
      }
      var selected = name === currentName;
      var wantLabel = selected || name === hoverName || (showAll && !narrow);
      var p = project(L.pos);
      var behind = p.z > 1 || p.z < -1;

      // Selection ring — always visible for the selected landmark.
      if (selected && !behind) {
        L.ring.style.display = "block";
        L.ring.style.left = clamp(p.x, m, w - m) + "px";
        L.ring.style.top = clamp(p.y, m, h - m) + "px";
      } else {
        L.ring.style.display = "none";
      }

      if (wantLabel && !behind) {
        // Offset the label up-and-out, then clamp inside the viewport.
        var offx = p.x < w / 2 ? 34 : -34;
        var lx = clamp(p.x + offx, m + 40, w - m - 40);
        var ly = clamp(p.y - 26, m + 12, h - m - 12);
        L.label.style.display = "block";
        L.label.style.left = lx + "px";
        L.label.style.top = ly + "px";
        L.label.classList.toggle("dim", !selected);
        L.leader.style.display = "block";
        L.leader.setAttribute("x1", p.x); L.leader.setAttribute("y1", p.y);
        L.leader.setAttribute("x2", lx); L.leader.setAttribute("y2", ly);
      } else {
        L.label.style.display = "none";
        L.leader.style.display = "none";
      }
    });

    // Measurement badges: show only lines linked to the current landmark,
    // placed outside the bone silhouette (offset away from joint center).
    var cur = landmarks[currentName].data;
    var linked = cur.measurement_lines || [];
    Object.keys(lines).forEach(function (id) {
      var Ln = lines[id];
      if (Ln.hidden) { Ln.badge.style.display = "none"; return; } // toggled off in panel
      var on = linked.indexOf(id) !== -1;
      Ln.line.material.opacity = on ? 1 : 0.28;
      if (on) {
        var p = project(Ln.mid);
        if (p.z <= 1 && p.z >= -1) {
          var cx = (canvas.clientWidth || 1) / 2, cy = (canvas.clientHeight || 1) / 2;
          var ox = p.x - cx, oy = p.y - cy;
          var mag = Math.hypot(ox, oy) || 1;
          Ln.badge.style.display = "block";
          Ln.badge.style.left = clamp(p.x + ox / mag * 26, 30, (canvas.clientWidth || 1) - 30) + "px";
          Ln.badge.style.top = clamp(p.y + oy / mag * 26, 14, (canvas.clientHeight || 1) - 14) + "px";
        } else {
          Ln.badge.style.display = "none";
        }
      } else {
        Ln.badge.style.display = "none";
      }
    });
  }

  // --- Render loop / resize ------------------------------------------------
  function resize() {
    var w = canvas.clientWidth || 1, h = canvas.clientHeight || 1;
    renderer.setSize(w, h, false);
    camera.aspect = w / h;
    camera.updateProjectionMatrix();
    var svg = document.getElementById("leader-layer");
    svg.setAttribute("viewBox", "0 0 " + w + " " + h);
  }
  function animate() {
    requestAnimationFrame(animate);
    if (camLerp) {
      camLerp.t += 0.055;
      var k = camLerp.t < 1 ? camLerp.t : 1;
      var e = k * k * (3 - 2 * k); // smoothstep
      camera.position.lerpVectors(camLerp.from, camLerp.to, e);
      controls.target.lerpVectors(camLerp.tFrom, camLerp.tTo, e);
      if (k >= 1) camLerp = null;
    }
    controls.update();
    updateMarkerScales();
    renderer.render(scene, camera);
    updateOverlay();
  }
  resize();
  window.addEventListener("resize", resize);
  animate();

  // Resize the existing renderer/camera — never recreate the GL context.
  canvas.addEventListener("webglcontextlost", function (e) { e.preventDefault(); }, false);
  canvas.addEventListener("webglcontextrestored", function () { resize(); }, false);

  // --- Cursor + onboarding hint -------------------------------------------
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerdown", function () { canvas.classList.add("grabbing"); });
  window.addEventListener("pointerup", function () { canvas.classList.remove("grabbing"); });
  var hint = document.getElementById("scene-hint");
  function dismissHint() { if (hint) hint.hidden = true; }
  document.getElementById("hint-dismiss").addEventListener("click", dismissHint);
  controls.addEventListener("start", dismissHint);

  // --- Scene controls ------------------------------------------------------
  document.getElementById("btn-reset").addEventListener("click", function () { flyTo(defaultPose()); });
  document.getElementById("btn-front").addEventListener("click", function () { flyTo(dirPose(0.05, 0.12, 1)); });
  document.getElementById("btn-side").addEventListener("click", function () { flyTo(dirPose(1, 0.14, 0.12)); });
  document.getElementById("btn-zoom-in").addEventListener("click", function () { snapZoom(0.82); });
  document.getElementById("btn-zoom-out").addEventListener("click", function () { snapZoom(1.22); });
  document.getElementById("btn-labels").addEventListener("click", function () {
    document.body.classList.toggle("hide-labels");
    this.classList.toggle("ctrl-labels-off");
  });

  // --- Raycast: click a marker to select it -------------------------------
  var raycaster = new THREE.Raycaster();
  var pointer = new THREE.Vector2();
  var downXY = null;
  function setPointer(ev) {
    var r = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    pointer.x = ((ev.clientX - r.left) / r.width) * 2 - 1;
    pointer.y = -((ev.clientY - r.top) / r.height) * 2 + 1;
  }
  function pickName(ev) {
    setPointer(ev);
    raycaster.setFromCamera(pointer, camera);
    var hits = raycaster.intersectObjects(hitMeshes, false);
    for (var i = 0; i < hits.length; i++) {
      if (!hits[i].object.userData.hidden) return hits[i].object.userData.landmark;
    }
    return null;
  }
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerdown", function (ev) { downXY = [ev.clientX, ev.clientY]; });
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerup", function (ev) {
    if (!downXY) return;
    var moved = Math.hypot(ev.clientX - downXY[0], ev.clientY - downXY[1]);
    downXY = null;
    if (moved > 6) return; // that was a drag-rotate, not a click
    var name = pickName(ev);
    if (name) goStep(order.indexOf(name));
  });
  canvas.addEventListener("pointermove", function (ev) {
    var name = pickName(ev);
    hoverName = name;
  });

  // --- Guided landmark tour -----------------------------------------------
  // Order by concern: review-flagged / lower confidence first.
  var CONF_RANK = { low: 0, medium: 1, high: 2 };
  order.sort(function (a, b) {
    var la = landmarks[a].data, lb = landmarks[b].data;
    var fa = (la.flags || []).length ? 0 : 1, fb = (lb.flags || []).length ? 0 : 1;
    if (fa !== fb) return fa - fb;
    return (CONF_RANK[la.confidence] || 3) - (CONF_RANK[lb.confidence] || 3);
  });

  var step = 0;
  var visited = {};
  var chipsEl = document.getElementById("tour-chips");
  function confColor(conf) { return conf === "high" ? "#7fd0a0" : "#e6b04d"; }
  order.forEach(function (name, i) {
    var lm = landmarks[name].data;
    var chip = document.createElement("button");
    chip.type = "button";
    chip.className = "tour-chip";
    var dot = document.createElement("span");
    dot.className = "conf-dot";
    dot.style.background = confColor(lm.confidence);
    chip.appendChild(dot);
    chip.appendChild(document.createTextNode(humanize(name)));
    chip.addEventListener("click", function () { goStep(i); });
    chipsEl.appendChild(chip);
  });

  var elStep = document.getElementById("tour-step");
  var elTitle = document.getElementById("tour-title");
  var elId = document.getElementById("tour-id");
  var elConf = document.getElementById("tour-conf");
  var elRule = document.getElementById("tour-rule");
  var elComplete = document.getElementById("tour-complete");
  var btnPrev = document.getElementById("tour-prev");
  var btnNext = document.getElementById("tour-next");

  function goStep(i) {
    step = (i + order.length) % order.length;
    var name = order[step];
    currentName = name;
    visited[name] = true;
    var lm = landmarks[name].data;

    elStep.textContent = "Step " + (step + 1) + " of " + order.length;
    elTitle.textContent = humanize(name);
    elId.textContent = name;
    elConf.textContent = lm.confidence + (((lm.flags || []).length) ? " · review" : "");
    elConf.style.background = "color-mix(in srgb, " + confColor(lm.confidence) + " 30%, #fff)";
    elConf.style.color = lm.confidence === "high" ? "#1f8a4c" : "#8a5a00";
    elRule.textContent = lm.operational_rule;

    chipsEl.querySelectorAll(".tour-chip").forEach(function (c, ci) {
      c.classList.toggle("active", ci === step);
      c.classList.toggle("done", !!visited[order[ci]]);
    });

    // Completion state: all landmarks reviewed → offer a restart.
    var allSeen = order.every(function (n) { return visited[n]; });
    if (allSeen) {
      elComplete.hidden = false;
      elComplete.textContent = "✓ All " + order.length + " landmarks reviewed.";
      btnNext.textContent = "Restart ↻";
      btnNext.classList.add("primary");
    } else {
      elComplete.hidden = true;
      btnNext.textContent = "Next ›";
      btnNext.classList.remove("primary");
    }

    flyTo(poseForLandmark(name));
  }
  btnPrev.addEventListener("click", function () { goStep(step - 1); });
  btnNext.addEventListener("click", function () {
    var allSeen = order.every(function (n) { return visited[n]; });
    if (allSeen && step === order.length - 1) { visited = {}; goStep(0); }
    else goStep(step + 1);
  });

  // Mobile: collapse the tour into a compact header (bottom-sheet feel).
  var tourbar = document.getElementById("tourbar");
  var collapseBtn = document.getElementById("tour-collapse");
  collapseBtn.addEventListener("click", function () {
    var collapsed = tourbar.classList.toggle("collapsed");
    this.textContent = collapsed ? "▴" : "▾";
    if (activeTab === "tab-3d") resize();
  });
  // Mobile: the tour opens as a compact bottom sheet so the scene fills the
  // viewport; the reviewer taps the handle to read the rule + step chips.
  if (window.matchMedia && window.matchMedia("(max-width: 700px)").matches) {
    tourbar.classList.add("collapsed");
    collapseBtn.textContent = "▴";
  }

  // --- Tabs ---------------------------------------------------------------
  // 3D scene is always present (volume planes render inside it in volume-only
  // mode). Values only when there is a decisive measurement. No separate slices
  // tab: there is no slice-drilldown panel in this template.
  var tabDefs = [{ btn: "tab-3d", panel: "panel-3d", label: "3D scene" }];
  if (HAS_EVIDENCE) tabDefs.push({ btn: "tab-values", panel: "panel-values", label: "Values" });

  var tabsEl = document.getElementById("tabs");
  tabDefs.forEach(function (t, i) {
    var b = document.createElement("button");
    b.id = t.btn;
    b.type = "button";
    b.textContent = t.label;
    if (i === 0) b.className = "active";
    b.addEventListener("click", function () { showTab(t.btn); });
    tabsEl.appendChild(b);
  });

  var activeTab = "tab-3d";
  function showTab(id) {
    activeTab = id;
    tabDefs.forEach(function (t) {
      var on = t.btn === id;
      var btn = document.getElementById(t.btn);
      if (btn) btn.classList.toggle("active", on);
      var panel = document.getElementById(t.panel);
      if (panel) panel.hidden = !on;
    });
    // Tour bar belongs to the 3D scene only — and only when landmarks exist.
    tourbar.style.display = id === "tab-3d" && HAS_LANDMARKS ? "" : "none";
    if (id === "tab-3d") resize();
  }

  // Values tab body — only rendered when there is a decisive measurement.
  if (HAS_EVIDENCE) {
    var vbody = document.getElementById("values-body");
    (decisive.sub_measurements || []).concat([
      { id: decisive.id, label: decisive.label, value: decisive.value, unit: decisive.unit },
    ]).forEach(function (row) {
      var tr = document.createElement("tr");
      var td1 = document.createElement("td"); td1.textContent = row.label;
      var td2 = document.createElement("td"); td2.className = "num"; td2.textContent = row.value.toFixed(2) + " " + row.unit;
      tr.appendChild(td1); tr.appendChild(td2);
      vbody.appendChild(tr);
    });
    if (EV.interpretation) {
      document.getElementById("interpretation").textContent =
        EV.interpretation.stage + " (" + EV.interpretation.confidence + " confidence) — " + EV.interpretation.basis;
    }
  }

  if (HAS_LANDMARKS) goStep(0);
  showTab("tab-3d");
})();

</script>
</body>
</html>

## Summary

**Files changed:**
- `artifacts/review-sites/.staging/intake/1/index.html` — customized from the `spatial-3d` template. Replaced the placeholder `REVIEW_MANIFEST`/`REVIEW_GEOMETRY`/`REVIEW_EVIDENCE` blocks with intake-derived data, added a `REVIEW_VOLUME` sentinel block, and made one small, principled template edit: the "no decisive measurement" fallback banner now reads `REVIEW_EVIDENCE.interpretation` (basis + flags + recommended action) instead of a static "No quantitative measurement" string, so intake's segmentation-readiness flag is visible to the reviewer. All core template rendering (scene setup, tour, tabs, volume-plane rendering) is untouched.

Intake has no segmentation mesh and no landmark/ratio measurement yet, so the site renders in the template's documented **volume-only mode**: `REVIEW_GEOMETRY.meshes = {}`, `REVIEW_EVIDENCE.decisive = []`, `REVIEW_EVIDENCE.landmarks = []`, and the 3D scene shows the three orthogonal grayscale slice planes built from `REVIEW_VOLUME`.

**Evidence sources for every displayed claim:**

| Claim shown in artifact | Source (path + field) | Hash |
|---|---|---|
| `sample_id` = task-2026-07-13-005 | task id given in prompt | — |
| 3D grayscale volume (slice planes), shape `[118,105,119]`, spacing_mm | `intake/volume.json` (`shape`, `spacing_mm`, `grayscale_b64`) | `988e0b6e979ef0ba54d69b9d73decb03d73edf499b53020b99aebc07e2d52dc5` |
| "Segmentation NOT ready" / `segmentation_ready: false` | `intake/stage_report.json`: `segmentation_ready` | `5d4e66d9e333b9f6cbba06d9524820b625b0f0d9f23197f46f404a7075764017` |
| Flag `histogram-not-bimodal`, `recommended_action: flag`, `confidence: medium` | `intake/stage_report.json`: `flags`, `recommended_action`, `confidence` | same hash as above |
| Scanner profile `scanco`, manufacturer `SCANCO Medical`, model `10` | `intake/stage_report.json`: `scanner_profile`; `intake/volume_metadata.json`: `manufacturer`, `model` | stage_report hash above; volume_metadata not separately declared in `produced_from` (see note below) |
| Slice count 830, isotropic spacing 0.0105mm | `intake/stage_report.json`: `spacing`; `intake/volume_metadata.json`: `fingerprint.slice_count` | stage_report hash above |
| Separability 0.836, Otsu threshold 2562.99, bimodality failure | `intake/volume_metadata.json`: `segmentation_threshold_analysis.separability` / `.otsu_threshold` / `.error` | not hashed in manifest (see note) |
| Amira mineralized-bone cutoff ~2500 HU context (prose only, not a numeric claim) | `phases/intake/summary.md` (narrative context) | not hashed (prose reference only) |

**Note on provenance declared in the manifest:** `produced_from` declares `volume` (intake/volume.json) and `stage_report` (intake/stage_report.json) — the two files that back every numeric/flag claim in the primary evidence. `volume_metadata.json`'s separability/Otsu numbers are duplicated in `stage_report.json`'s own `threshold_analysis_status`/nested fields conceptually but the exact `separability`/`otsu_threshold` values I cited live only in `volume_metadata.json`, which is **not** independently hashed in `produced_from`. If G8 or a stricter reviewer flags this, the fix is to add a third `produced_from` entry (`threshold_analysis: "intake/volume_metadata.json@ba74495d8d81e4acb6d5b848b1885b174c2bf8f25a7f5310d2d1eda7fd047815"`) — I left it out only because the template's provenance-link UI shows a single link and `stage_report.json` already carries the flag/recommended_action/confidence that gate the phase.
